title: Father came from Carolina to Dayton and Genesee and worked on farm instead of homesteading
description: Father came from Carolina to Dayton and then Genesee. Rather than homestead in the timber, he worked on a farm. He bought land without even walking on it; his gradual accumulation of land from Indians. Father's banjo. He came in late 1880's.

title: People travelled little and made so with what they had at home
description: There was little travelling around then; people made do with what they had at home. A lady came from town to make soap. Butchering. Progress to machines from stock.

title: Explanation of why father used mules
description: Why father used mules instead of horses - less worry about handling by the men. "Cold shouldered" horses and horse trading. Acquiring mules.

title: Father's farming operation, borrowed from bank to expand; partnership with Mr. Driscoll
description: Pictures of the operation. Father a good manager, and building up was slow. Borrowing from the bank to expand. Partnership with Mr. Driscoll, who owned Genesee hardware store and financed his father's management. Each owned a thousand acres until the thirties when their children took over. Father gave instructions; but did not do the work himself. Pressure of production in partnership; father's offer to buy out Driscoll's share of a crop.

title: Father's wage for managing; his farm work as a boy
description: Father's wage for managing. Working during threshing was disciplined. His farm work as a boy. As a partnership he was paid as an incentive.

title: Size of farm crew compared to transients; many of men came out of Carolina; father acted as foreman
description: Size of farm crew. They contracted for threshing. Local crews compared to transients. Getting transients from the jungle at Genesee; they were often problems. Many of his local men had come out from Carolina. Father acted as foreman. The men worked well for him; they had pride in their work, and had to make a living. IWW threat never materialized in the fields; they didn't want transient help to become disgruntled.

title: Need for close management of the operation
description: Need for close management of the operation. Difference between his set-up and that of most people. His operation the largest in the area.

title: Buying young pigs from the small farmers around Enterprise
description: Buying young pigs from the small farmers around Enterprise and elsewhere, to be turned out on the waste in the fields. Grazing shepherds on summer fallow and wheat foliage.

title: Hogs harvested the peas; rotation of crops; plowing and haying; small rodeos
description: Hogs harvested the peas. Peas began to be raised in the thirties, Rotation of fall wheat, fall barley and summer fallow. Time of planting and harvesting. Plowing was slow work. Haying at the beginning of July, hoping to get off for the fourth. Small local rodeos. The tremendous change from early years; can it continue to change as fast?

title: Chautauqua activities; Genesee horse shows
description: Chautauqua activities, the shows; heaving to sell their tickets at a loss in bad years.~ Genesee horse show was for showing horses. His father and others had to underwrite

title: Moving into town so children could go to high school; school activities and entertainment
description: Playing in straw stacks. Moving into town in the fall so the children could go to high school. Social activities at Jain School were only entertainment for country folks; dancing gradually switched to town. Parents had little involvement in town social life.

title: Serious interest in singing at the University; sang at night clubs in San Francisco but gave it up as career
description: His serious interest in singing at the University. He sang in night clubs in San Francisco after graduating, but decided to give it up as a career, (continued)

title: Opportunities for singing in the area.
description: Opportunities for singing in the area.

title: Aggressiveness of farmers and presence of University Inventions by local farmers patented
description: Aggressiveness of farmers related to presence of University Inventions by local farmers were later patented by national companies. The innovations in machinery came from the farmers because of the incentive to improve. Mechanization through time.

title: His study of agricultural engineering
description: His study of agricultural engineering was useful orientation, but not practical in itself. He took over farm in 1938.
